_comment: Drop this into any Element 22 product repo as .claude/settings.json. When teammates trust the project folder, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, and Claude.ai (Chat) will prompt them to install the e22-plugins marketplace. This file IS meant to be committed; it is per-project, not per-user. User-specific overrides go in .claude/settings.local.json which is gitignored.

enabledPlugins._comment_always_on: The four always-on plugins. PO bundle AND Dev bundle install these. e22-org is the load-bearing one — it ships the always-loaded CLAUDE.md, the sandbox guardrails, the natural-language handoff trigger, and the shared lib/zone.sh that the production plugins source.
enabledPlugins._comment_production: The three production-only plugins. Zone-gated — silent in the local MVP sandbox, full enforcement when the workspace is a git repo with a GitHub remote. Engineers want these enabled. POs can keep them enabled too — they're no-ops in the sandbox so they don't add noise. Disable any individual one in .claude/settings.local.json if needed.

_comment_connectors: GitHub is required only for the governed-production zone. The local MVP sandbox (PO exploration) is connector-free. Recommended: Sentry, Statsig (accessed via the OpenFeature SDK for /promote), context7. See CONNECTORS.md in the marketplace repo for the full reference. The keys below are documentary metadata, not validated settings.

notes.statsig: Accessed through the OpenFeature SDK so the flag platform stays swappable.
